results.possibleIdentity: Paul "Chris" Setzler (1935–2019), also known as Paul Christie Setzler, was a mechanical engineer and inventor active in the late 1960s and 1970s. He earned a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from Purdue University and was employed by the Heli-Coil Corporation in Danbury, Connecticut, while residing in Sherman, Connecticut. Setzler is best known for developing and patenting the "Spinserter," an automatic power tool used to install coiled wire inserts. His invention was honored by the editors of Industrial Design magazine in 1968 and was subsequently displayed at the Smithsonian Institution. His technical expertise spanned a wide range of fields, including mechanical engineering, hydraulics, fluidics, pneumatics, fiberglass, plastics/polymers, elastomers, and instrumentation and control systems. He was associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, where his skills were registered to facilitate collaborations with artists. In his later years, Setzler moved to Virginia and became a fine woodworker specializing in post-and-beam construction and Shaker-style furniture, while also researching alternative energy sources such as windmills.
